title: chenopodium vulvaria
intro: Chenopodium vulvaria, also known as the common wormwood or lamb's quarters, is a plant species in the Chenopodium genus. It has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, offering health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Chenopodium vulvaria can be prepared as a tea, infused in oil, or made into a salve for topical application, and its leaves can also be used in salads for added nutrition.
